front: Depois de correr tanto atrás do ônibus, acabei perdendo a viagem e fiquei a ver navios.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee kohr-REHR TAN-too ah-TRAHZ doh OH-nee-booz, ah-kah-BEE pehr-DEHN-doh ah VYAH-zheng ee fee-KAY ah VEHR nah-VEE-ooz.
back: After running so hard after the bus, I ended up missing it and was left empty-handed.
context: Using 'ficar a ver navios' (to be left watching ships) to mean being left disappointed, empty-handed, or empty-promised.

front: Ele adora soltar piadinhas na reunião para ver se consegue tirar o foco do erro dele.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah sohl-TAHR pyah-JEENG-yahz nah rrehoo-nee-ONG PAH-rah VEHR see kohn-SEH-ghee tee-RAHR oh FOH-koo doh_EHR-roo DEH-lee.
back: He loves to make little jokes in the meeting to see if he can draw attention away from his mistake.
context: Using 'soltar piadinha' to mean making subtle, sometimes passive-aggressive or distracting jokes.

front: Achei que o processo de visto seria demorado, mas deu tudo certo e foi um mamão com açúcar.
pronunciation: ah-SHAY keh oh proh-SEHS-soo jee VEES-too seh-REE-ah jee-moh-RAH-doh, mahz DEW TOO-doh SEHR-too ee FOY oong mah-MONG kohm ah-SOO-kahr.
back: I thought the visa process would take long, but everything worked out and it was a piece of cake.
context: Using 'mamão com açúcar' (papaya with sugar) to describe something extremely easy, smooth, or effortless.

front: Ela tentou dar uma de santa quando a mãe descobriu a travessura, mas não colou.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH dahr OO-mah jee SAn-tah KWAHN-doh ah MONG dees-koh-BREW ah trah-veh-SOO-rah, mahz NOHNG koh-LOH.
back: She tried to play the innocent one when her mother found out about the prank, but it didn't work.
context: Using 'dar uma de santa' (to play the saint/innocent) and 'não colar' (to not stick/not be believable).

front: O preço do aluguel naquela região está os olhos da cara; é impossível morar lá.
pronunciation: oh PREH-soo doh ah-loo-GHEL nah-KEH-lyah rreh-zhee-ONG ees-TAH ooz OH-lyoohz dah KAH-rah; eh eeng-poh-SEE-vehl moh-RAHR LAH.
back: The rent prices in that region cost an arm and a leg; it's impossible to live there.
context: Using 'custar os olhos da cara' (to cost the eyes of the face) to mean extremely expensive.

front: Não adianta tentar me enrolar; eu conheço esse truque de trás para frente.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah tehn-TAHR mee ehn-rro-LAHR; ew kohn-YAY-soo EHS-see TROO-kee jee TRAHZ PAH-rah FREHN-chee.
back: There is no use trying to stall or trick me; I know this trick inside out.
context: Using 'conhecer de trás para frente' (to know from back to front) to mean knowing something thoroughly or inside out.

front: Ele sempre tenta carregar o mundo nas costas e acaba ficando doente de tanto estresse.
pronunciation: EH-lee SEHM-pree TEHN-tah kahr-rreh-GAHR oh MOON-doo nahz KOHS-tahz ee ah-KAH-bah fee-KAHN-doh do-EHN-chee jee TAN-too ees-TREHS-see.
back: He always tries to carry the weight of the world on his shoulders and ends up getting sick from so much stress.
context: Using 'carregar o mundo nas costas' to describe taking on too much responsibility or over-burdening oneself.

front: A ideia de mudar as regras do condomínio de última hora deu o que falar na assembleia.
pronunciation: ah ee-DAY-ah jee moo-DAHR ahz RRAY-grahz doh kohn-doh-MEE-nyoo jee OOL-chee-mah OH-rah DEW oh keh fah-LAHR nah ah-sehm-BLEH-yah.
back: The idea of changing the condo rules at the last minute caused quite a stir in the meeting.
context: Using 'dar o que falar' (to give what to talk about) to mean causing gossip, controversy, or making headlines.

front: Não compre essa marca de ferramentas baratas, senão você vai acabar quebrando a cara.
pronunciation: NOHNG KOHM-pree EHS-see MAHR-kah jee fehr-rrah-MEHN-tahz bah-RAH-tahz, see-NONG voh-SEH VAY ah-kah-BAHR kweh-BRAHN-doh ah KAH-rah.
back: Don't buy this cheap brand of tools, otherwise you will end up disappointed.
context: Using 'quebrar a cara' (to break one's face) to mean failing, getting hurt, or being deeply disappointed after high expectations.

front: Ela resolveu arregaçar as mangas e limpar toda a bagunça que os inquilinos deixaram.
pronunciation: EH-lyah rreh-sohl-VEW ah-rreh-gah-SAHR ahz MAN-gahz ee leeng-PAHR TOH-dah ah bah-GOON-sah keh ooz eeng-kee-LEE-noohz day-SHAH-roong.
back: She decided to roll up her sleeves and clean up all the mess the tenants left behind.
context: A practical domestic cleaning context for the idiom 'arregaçar as mangas'.

front: Achei que ia perder o dia inteiro na fila do banco, mas para minha surpresa foi rapidinho.
pronunciation: ah-SHAY keh EE-ah pehr-DEHR oh DEE-ah een-TAY-roo nah FEE-lyah doh BAN-koo, mahz PAH-rah MEENG-yah soor-PREH-zah FOY rrah-pee-JEE-nyoo.
back: I thought I was going to waste the entire day in the bank line, but to my surprise it was super quick.
context: Using 'rapidinho' as the common diminutive form of 'rápido' used to emphasize a very short amount of time.

front: Ele adora colocar lenha na fogueira quando vê uma discussão de família começar.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah koh-loh-KAHR LEH-nyah nah foh-GAY-rah KWAHN-doh VEH OO-mah dees-koo-SOHNG jee fah-MEE-lyah koh-meh-SAHR.
back: He loves to add fuel to the fire when he sees a family argument starting.
context: Using 'colocar lenha na fogueira' (to put firewood on the bonfire) to mean making an argument or tense situation worse.

front: Nossa equipe estava quase desistindo, mas o novo desenvolvedor conseguiu salvar a pátria.
pronunciation: NOHS-sah ee-KEE-pee ees-TAH-vah KWAH-zee dez-ees-CHEENG-doh, mahz oh NOH-voo deez-eheng-vohl-vee-DOHR kohn-seh-GHEW sahl-VAHR ah PAH-tryah.
back: Our team was almost giving up, but the new developer managed to save the day.
context: Using 'salvar a pátria' (to save the homeland) colloquially to mean saving the day or resolving a critical problem.

front: Ela tentou me fazer de gato e sapato, mas eu percebi a tempo e saí daquela relação.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH mee fah-ZEHR jee GAH-too ee sah-PAH-too, mahz ew pehr-seh-BEE ah TEHM-poo ee sah-EE dah-KEH-lyah rreh-lah-SOHNG.
back: She tried to walk all over me, but I noticed in time and got out of that relationship.
context: Using 'fazer de gato e sapato' (to make [someone] into a cat and a shoe) to mean treating someone poorly, manipulating, or walking all over them.

front: Não adianta ficar de braços cruzados esperando que a solução caia do céu.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah fee-KAHR jee BRAH-soohz kroo-ZAH-doohz ees-peh-RAHN-doh keh ah soh-loo-SOHNG KY-ah doh SEW.
back: It's no use sitting idly by waiting for the solution to fall from the sky.
context: Using 'ficar de braços cruzados' (to stay with crossed arms) to mean doing nothing or being passive in the face of a problem.

front: Fui dar uma de sabichão ao tentar consertar o encanamento e acabei inundando a cozinha.
pronunciation: FWEE dahr OO-mah jee sah-bee-SHONG AH-oo tehn-TAHR kohn-sehr-TAHR oh eheng-kah-nah-MEHN-too ee ah-kah-BEE ee-noon-DAHN-doh ah koh-ZEENG-yah.
back: I tried to act like a know-it-all when trying to fix the plumbing and ended up flooding the kitchen.
context: Using 'dar uma de sabichão' to mean acting like a smarty-pants or a know-it-all.

front: Ele ficou sem ação e engoliu em seco quando o chefe pediu explicações sobre o atraso.
pronunciation: EH-lee fee-KOH sehng ah-SOHNG ee eheng-goh-LEW ehng SEH-koo KWAHN-doh oh SHEH-fee peh-JEW ees-plee-kah-SOHNG_Z SOH-bree oh ah-TRAH-zoo.
back: He was left speechless and swallowed hard when the boss asked for explanations about the delay.
context: Using 'engolir em seco' (to swallow dry) to mean swallowing one's pride, holding back emotions, or reacting with nervous silence.

front: Ela tem uma sorte incrível; parece que nasceu com a bunda virada para lua.
pronunciation: EH-lyah teheng OO-mah SOHR-chee eeng-KREE-vehl; pah-REH-see keh nah-SEW kohm ah BOON-dah vee-RAH-dah PAH-rah LOO-ah.
back: She has incredible luck; it seems like she was born under a lucky star.
context: Using 'nascer com a bunda virada para a lua' (to be born with one's backside facing the moon) as an extremely common Brazilian idiom for being exceptionally lucky.

front: Depois de tantas tentativas frustradas de resolver o bug, decidi dar um tempo e arejar a cabeça.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee TAN-tah_z tehn-tah-CHEE-vahz froos-TRAH-dahz jee rreh-sohl-VEHR oh BUG, jee-see-JEE dahr oong TEHM-poo ee ah-reh-ZHAHR ah kah-BEH-sah.
back: After so many frustrated attempts to resolve the bug, I decided to take a break and clear my head.
context: Using 'arejar a cabeça' (to air out the head) to mean clearing one's mind or taking a mental break.

front: Não perca a cabeça por causa de discussões bobas nas redes sociais.
pronunciation: NOHNG PEHR-kah ah kah-BEH-sah pohr KOW-zah jee dees-koo-SOHNG_Z BOH-bahz nah_z RREH-jeez soh-SYAY_Z.
back: Don't lose your temper over silly arguments on social media.
context: Using 'perder a cabeça' (to lose one's head) to mean losing control of one's temper, patience, or rationality.

front: Ele adora dar uma de João sem braço quando é a vez dele de lavar a louça.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah dahr OO-mah jee zhoh-ONG sehng BRAH-soo KWAHN-doh eh ah VEH_Z DEH-lee jee lah-VAHR ah LOW-sah.
back: He loves to play dumb and avoid the task when it's his turn to wash the dishes.
context: Another everyday domestic application of 'dar uma de João sem braço' (playing dumb to shirk responsibility).

front: Ela fala pelos cotovelos; se você não interromper, ela não para de falar um segundo.
pronunciation: EH-lyah FAH-lyah PEH-loohz koh-toh-VEH-loohz; see voh-SEH NOHNG eeng-tehr-rrohm-PEHR, EH-lyah NOHNG PAH-rah jee fah-LAHR oong seh-GOON-doo.
back: She talks non-stop; if you don't interrupt, she won't stop talking for a second.
context: Using 'falar pelos cotovelos' (to talk through one's elbows) to describe someone who is extremely talkative or chatty.

front: Ele é muito liso; sempre consegue escapar de situações difíceis sem dar explicações.
pronunciation: EH-lee eh MWEE-too LEE-zoo; SEHM-pree kohn-SEH-ghee ees-kah-PAHR jee see-twah-SOHNG_Z jee-FEE-seeez sehng dahr ees-plee-kah-SOHNG_Z.
back: He is very slippery; he always manages to escape difficult situations without giving explanations.
context: Using 'liso' (smooth/slippery) to describe a cunning person who easily avoids blame, commitments, or tricky situations.

front: Achei que o jantar seria simples, mas a comida estava de comer rezando.
pronunciation: ah-SHAY keh oh zhan-TAHR seh-REE-ah SEENG-pleez, mahz ah koh-MEE-dah ees-TAH-vah jee koh-MEHR rreh-ZAHN-doh.
back: I thought the dinner would be simple, but the food was absolutely divine.
context: Using 'de comer rezando' (to eat praying) to describe food that is exceptionally delicious.

front: Não adianta tentar dar nó em pingo d'água; você precisa seguir as instruções de instalação.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah tehn-TAHR dahr NOH ehng PEENG-goo DAH-gwah; voh-SEH pree-SEE-zah seh-GHEER ahz eengs-troo-SOHNG_Z jee eengs-tah-lah-SOHNG.
back: There is no use trying to work miracles or find loopholes; you need to follow the installation instructions.
context: Applying 'dar nó em pingo d'água' to a technical assembly/installation context.

front: Fiquei com o coração na mão ao ver meu celular cair de uma altura enorme.
pronunciation: fee-KAY kohm oh koh-rah-SOHNG nah MONG AH-oo VEHR MEW seh-loo-LAHR kah-EER jee OO-mah owl-TOO-rah ee-NOHR-mee.
back: My heart was in my mouth when I saw my cell phone fall from a huge height.
context: Using 'com o coração na mão' (with the heart in one's hand) to express extreme anxiety, worry, or fear about an ongoing event.

front: Ela vive metendo os pés pelas mãos quando tenta fazer várias coisas ao mesmo tempo.
pronunciation: EH-lyah VEE-vee meh-TEHN-doh ooz PEH_Z PEH-lahz MAHEEZ KWAHN-doh TEHN-tah fah-ZEHR VAH-ryahz KOY-zahz AH-oo MEHS-moo TEHM-poo.
back: She constantly gets her wires crossed and makes mistakes when she tries to multitask.
context: Using 'meter os pés pelas mãos' (to put feet through hands) to describe acting in a clumsy, confused, or disorganized way, often messing up.

front: O evento de tecnologia foi um divisor de águas na minha jornada de estudos de programação.
pronunciation: oh ee-VEHN-toh jee chehk-noh-loh-ZHEE-ah FOY oong jee-vee-ZOHR jee AH-gwahz nah MEENG-yah zhor-NAH-dah jee ees-TOO-doohz jee proh-grah-mah-SOHNG.
back: The tech event was a turning point in my programming study journey.
context: Using 'divisor de águas' in an educational/career context.

front: Ele adora vender gato por lebre, mas na internet as pessoas descobrem a verdade rápido.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah vehn-DEHR GAH-too pohr LEH-bree, mahz nah eeng-tehr-NEHT az peh-SOH-ahz dees-KOH-breheng ah vehr-DAH-jee RRAH-pee-doh.
back: He loves to rip people off, but on the internet people find out the truth quickly.
context: Using 'vender gato por lebre' to denote online scams, misleading listings, or dishonest sales.

front: Não gosto de sair com quem só quer fazer fita e mostrar um estilo de vida irreal.
pronunciation: NOHNG GOHS-too jee sah-EER kohm KEHNG SOH KEHR fah-ZEHR FEE-tah ee mohs-TRAHR oong ees-CHEE-loo jee VEE-dah ee-rre-AHL.
back: I don't like going out with people who just want to show off and display an unrealistic lifestyle.
context: Using 'fazer fita' (to make a ribbon/film tape) to mean showing off, pretending, posing, or acting artificially.

front: Depois de perder o emprego, ele teve que rebolar para pagar as contas em dia.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee pehr-DEHR oh ehem-PREH-goo, EH-lee CHEE-vee keh rreh-boh-LAHR PAH-rah pah-GAHR ahz KOHN-tahz ehng DEE-ah.
back: After losing his job, he had to scramble to pay his bills on time.
context: Using 'rebolar' to describe maneuvering, scrambling, or doing financial gymnastics to make ends meet.

front: Achei o comportamento dele muito estranho e fiquei com a pulga atrás da orelha.
pronunciation: ah-SHAY oh kohm-pohr-tah-MEHN-toh DEH-lee MWEE-too ees-TRAN-yoo ee fee-KAY kohm ah POOL-gah ah-TRAHZ dah oh-REHL-yah.
back: I found his behavior very strange and became highly suspicious.
context: Using 'com a pulga atrás da orelha' in a social behavioral context.

front: Ela tentou tapar o sol com a peneira inventando uma desculpa boba para o atraso.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH tah-PAHR oh SOHL kohm ah peh-NAY-rah eeng-vehn-TAHN-doh OO-mah dees-KOOL-pah BOH-bah PAH-rah oh ah-TRAH-zoo.
back: She tried to cover up the obvious by making up a silly excuse for being late.
context: Using 'tapar o sol com a peneira' to describe a weak, unconvincing excuse.

front: O novo fone de ouvido sem fio é uma mão na roda para treinar na academia.
pronunciation: oh NOH-voo FOH-nee jee oh-VEE-doo sehng FEE-oo eh OO-mah MONG nah RROH-dah PAH-rah tray-NAHR nah ah-kah-deh-MEE-ah.
back: The new wireless headphones are a lifesaver for working out at the gym.
context: Using 'uma mão na roda' (a hand on the wheel) to describe something highly convenient, helpful, or practical.

front: Ele adora chover no molhado, repetindo as mesmas dicas de estudo que todos já sabem.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah sho-VEHR noo moh-LYAH-doh, rreh-peh-CHEENG-doh az MEHS-mohz JEE-kahz jee ees-TOO-doo keh TOH-doohz zha SAH-beheng.
back: He loves to state the obvious, repeating the same study tips that everyone already knows.
context: Using 'chover no molhado' (to rain on wet ground) to describe saying or doing redundant things, stating the obvious, or repeating what has already been said.

front: Ela é muito cabeça-quente; qualquer coisinha é motivo para ela começar a brigar.
pronunciation: EH-lyah eh MWEE-too kah-BEH-sah KEHN-chee; kwahl-KEHR koy-ZEENG-yah eh moh-CHEE-voo PAH-rah EH-lyah koh-meh-SAHR ah bree-GAHR.
back: She is very hot-headed; any little thing is an excuse for her to start fighting.
context: Using 'cabeça-quente' (hot-headed) to describe someone who is easily angered, impatient, or quick-tempered.

front: Nossos planos de ir ao parque foram por água abaixo por causa da chuva forte.
pronunciation: NOHS-soohz PLAH-noohz jee EER AH-oo PAHR-kee FOH-roong pohr AH-gwah ah-BAY-shoo pohr KOW-zah dah SHOO-vah FOHR-chee.
back: Our plans to go to the park fell through because of the heavy rain.
context: Another recreational context for 'ir por água abaixo'.

front: Fui dar uma de espertinho tentando cortar caminho e acabei me perdendo no bairro novo.
pronunciation: FWEE dahr OO-mah jee ehs-pehr-CHEENG-yoo tehn-TAHN-doh kohr-TAHR kah-MEENG-yoo ee ah-kah-BEE mee pehr-DEHN-doh noo BAY-rroo NOH-voo.
back: I tried to be clever by taking a shortcut and ended up getting lost in the new neighborhood.
context: Using 'dar uma de espertinho' to describe trying to act smart or slyly outwit a situation, usually resulting in failure.

front: A reunião foi tensa, mas no final todos conseguiram manter a cabeça fria.
pronunciation: ah rrehoo-nee-ONG FOY TEHN-sah, mahz noo fee-NAHL TOH-doohz kohn-seh-GHEE-roong mhn-TEHR ah kah-BEH-sah FREE-ah.
back: The meeting was tense, but in the end everyone managed to keep a cool head.
context: Using 'manter a cabeça fria' (to keep a cold head) as the standard way to express keeping calm under pressure.

front: Não dê corda para os boatos do escritório se você quiser manter uma boa convivência.
pronunciation: NOHNG DEH KOHR-dah PAH-rah ooz boh-AH-toohz doh ees-kree-TOH-ryoo see voh-SEH kee-SEHR mhn-TEHR OO-mah BOH-ah kohn-vee-VEHN-syah.
back: Don't feed into office rumors if you want to maintain a good working relationship.
context: Using 'dar corda' to mean encouraging, validating, or feeding into gossips or rumors.

front: Ele adora meter a colher onde não é chamado e dar conselhos não solicitados.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah meh-TEHR ah koh-LYEHR OHN-jee NOHNG eh shah-MAH-doh ee dahr kohn-SEH-lyoohz NOHNG soh-lee-see-TAH-doohz.
back: He loves to meddle where he's not wanted and give unsolicited advice.
context: Using 'meter a colher' (to stick the spoon [in someone else's pot/plate]) to mean meddling or interfering in someone else's business.

front: Depois de tanto estudar Vue.js, finalmente sinto que matei a charada do desenvolvimento front-end.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee TAN-too ees-too-DAHR Vue.js, fee-nahl-MEHN-tee SEENG-too keh mah-TAY ah shah-RAH-dah doh deez-eheng-vohl-vee-MEHN-too front-end.
back: After studying Vue.js so much, I finally feel like I've cracked the code of front-end development.
context: A modern web development application of 'matar a charada' (to crack the code/solve the mystery).

front: Ela tentou esconder o erro, mas acabou entregando o ouro ao ficar muito nervosa.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH ees-kohn-DEHR oh EHR-roo, mahz ah-kah-BOH een-treh-GAHN-doh oh OW-roo AH-oo fee-KAHR MWEE-too nehr-VOH-zah.
back: She tried to hide the mistake, but ended up giving the game away by getting very nervous.
context: Using 'entregar o ouro' (to hand over the gold) to mean accidentally letting a secret slip or giving away an advantage.

front: A palestra dele foi tão boa que deixou todo mundo com os olhos brilhando.
pronunciation: ah pah-LEHS-trah DEH-lee FOY TOHNG BOH-ah keh day-SHOH TOH-doh MOON-doo kohm ooz OH-lyoohz bree-LYAHN-doh.
back: His lecture was so good that it left everyone with their eyes shining with excitement.
context: Using 'ficar com os olhos brilhando' (to have one's eyes shining) to describe someone being deeply inspired, excited, or full of hope.

front: Não compre esse curso sem ler os comentários para não dar um tiro no escuro.
pronunciation: NOHNG KOHM-pree EHS-see KOOR-soo sehng LEHR ooz koh-mehn-TAH-ryoohz PAH-rah NOHNG dahr oong CHEE-roo noo ees-KOO-roo.
back: Don't buy this course without reading the reviews so you don't take a shot in the dark.
context: Using 'dar um tiro no escuro' (to take a shot in the dark) to mean guessing, taking a complete risk, or making an uncalculated gamble.
